name: MongoDB Reader
summary: Polls a MongoDB collection using a configurable query and emits one event per document.
description:
# MongoDB Reader

Executes a MongoDB find query on a configurable interval and emits one `MongoDocument` event per matching document. References a saved connection from the FlowDSL runtime connection store.

## Use cases
- Poll for newly inserted or updated documents
- Trigger flows from database state changes
- Batch-read records for downstream processing

## Notes
- `connectionId` references a connection saved in the FlowDSL runtime or Studio connection manager
- `query` must be a valid JSON object expression (MongoDB query syntax)
- Documents are emitted individually, one per runtime tick
- To avoid re-processing, add a `lastProcessedAt` field to your documents and filter in the query

outputs.name: MongoDocument
outputs.description: Emitted once per document returned by the query. The full document is forwarded as the payload.

schema.description: A MongoDB document. Shape depends on the collection queried.

connectionId.title: Connection
connectionId.description: ID of a saved MongoDB connection in the runtime connection store.

collection.title: Collection
collection.description: Name of the MongoDB collection to query.

query.title: Query
query.description: MongoDB query expression as a JSON string. Leave empty to match all documents.

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of documents to retrieve per poll.

pollIntervalSec.title: Poll Interval (seconds)
pollIntervalSec.description: How often to run the query.
